steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has a problem: after the blank, 'the study examined' is an independent clause. Whatever fills the blank must create a grammatically correct boundary between 'United States' and 'the study examined.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'United States, led': a comma before 'led by the researcher Laura A. Burkle' creates a comma splice — two independent clauses joined only by a comma. ✗
  • Choice B — 'United States and led': 'and' joins two independent clauses without a comma before it, creating a run-on. ✗
  • Choice C — 'United States led': no punctuation at all between two independent clauses — a fused sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — 'United States. Led': a period ends the first independent clause; 'Led by the researcher Laura A. Burkle, the study examined...' is a grammatically correct new sentence with an introductory participial phrase followed by a comma. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
